Container.Name: Sure-AIO
Container.Overview:
Sure (formerly Maybe Finance) is a self-hosted personal finance app.
[b]All-In-One (AIO) Edition[/b] This template uses a custom AIO image that effortlessly consolidates the Rails Web Server and the Sidekiq Worker into a single container using s6-overlay.
[b]Required Dependencies:[/b] 1. Postgres 16+ (or pgvector image if using local AI document search) 2. Redis 5.4+
[b]Initial Setup:[/b] 1. Create a database named `sure` in your Postgres container. 2. Run `openssl rand -hex 64` in your Unraid terminal to generate your `SECRET_KEY_BASE`. 3. Click "Apply". On first boot, the app will setup the database schemas. 4. Visit the WebUI and click "Create Account".

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: The main web interface port.

Config.Name: App Storage
Config.Description: Persistent storage for file uploads and assets.

Config.Name: Secret Key Base
Config.Description: Critical: Run 'openssl rand -hex 64' in your terminal and paste the result here.

Config.Name: Database Host
Config.Description: Hostname or IP of your Postgres database.

Config.Name: Database Port
Config.Description: Port for your Postgres database.

Config.Name: Database Name
Config.Description: Name of the database to use.

Config.Name: Database User
Config.Description: Database username.

Config.Name: Database Password
Config.Description: Database password.

Config.Name: Redis URL
Config.Description: Full connection string for Redis (e.g., redis://host:port/db).

Config.Name: Onboarding State
Config.Description: Controls user registration. Use 'open', 'closed', or 'invite_only'.

Config.Name: Assume SSL
Config.Description: Set to 'true' if you are putting this behind a reverse proxy like Nginx Proxy Manager or Cloudflare Tunnels.

Config.Name: [AI] OpenAI / Ollama Token
Config.Description: API Key for OpenAI. If using a local Ollama container, enter 'ollama-local'.

Config.Name: [AI] OpenAI URI Base
Config.Description: Leave blank for official OpenAI. For local LLMs, enter your endpoint (e.g., 'http://ollama:11434/v1').

Config.Name: [AI] Model Name
Config.Description: If using Ollama, you MUST define the model here (e.g., 'llama3.1:13b' or 'gemma2:7b').

Config.Name: [AI] Vector Store Provider
Config.Description: Backend for document search. Options: 'openai', 'pgvector', 'qdrant'.

Config.Name: [Ext. AI] Assistant Type
Config.Description: Set to 'external' to route all chat to an external agent via MCP.

Config.Name: [Ext. AI] Assistant URL
Config.Description: URL for the external agent (e.g. https://your-openclaw/v1/chat/completions).

Config.Name: [Ext. AI] Assistant Token
Config.Description: Auth token for the external agent.

Config.Name: [API] Brandfetch Client ID
Config.Description: Brandfetch OAuth key to auto-grab logos for banks and merchants.

Config.Name: [API] Twelve Data Key
Config.Description: Optional API Key for Twelve Data (exchange rates/stocks) if you don't want to use the default free Yahoo Finance option.

Config.Name: [Email] SMTP Address
Config.Description: Hostname for your SMTP server.

Config.Name: [Email] SMTP Port
Config.Description: Port for your SMTP server.

Config.Name: [Email] SMTP Username
Config.Description: Username for your SMTP server.

Config.Name: [Email] SMTP Password
Config.Description: Password for your SMTP server.

Config.Name: [Email] Sender Address
Config.Description: The email address your app will send mail from (e.g., finance@mydomain.com).

Config.Name: [Auth] OIDC Client ID
Config.Description: OpenID Connect Client ID (Authentik/Authelia).

Config.Name: [Auth] OIDC Client Secret
Config.Description: OpenID Connect Client Secret.

Config.Name: [Auth] OIDC Issuer
Config.Description: OpenID Connect Issuer URL.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] Langfuse Host
Config.Description: Langfuse instance URL for LLM observability.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] Langfuse Public Key
Config.Description: Langfuse Public Key.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] Langfuse Secret Key
Config.Description: Langfuse Secret Key.

Config.Name: [DB] Active Record Primary Key
Config.Description: (Optional) Explicit DB encryption key. If blank, derives from SECRET_KEY_BASE.
